entities.name: GERMPLASM
entities.description: The raw material used for crop improvement programs and a biological heritage resource.

entities.name: CROP IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: Programs aimed at enhancing the characteristics of crops.

entities.name: PLANT GENETIC RESOURCES
entities.description: The sum total of diversity accumulated through evolution, domestication, and natural selection.

entities.name: DOMESTICATION
entities.description: The process of adapting wild plants for human use.

entities.name: NATURAL SELECTION
entities.description: The process whereby organisms better adapted to their environment tend to survive and produce more offspring.

entities.name: GENETIC MANIPULATION
entities.description: The process of altering the genetic makeup of an organism.

entities.name: GENETIC ENGINEERING
entities.description: Techniques used to manipulate an organism's genes directly.

entities.name: GENES
entities.description: The basic physical and functional unit of heredity.

entities.name: FARM PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of agricultural output per unit of input.

entities.name: INTERNATIONAL AGRICULTURAL RESEARCH CENTERS
entities.description: Institutions strategically located in regions of high crop diversity to collect and conserve germplasm.

entities.name: CROP DIVERSITY
entities.description: The variety of different crop species and genotypes within a region.
